title: Adrenal incidentaloma

investigations.text: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped incidentaloma identity, imaging-report, and qualified follow-up-imaging documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

exact_section.heading: Assessment of malignancy risk by imaging

investigations.text: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-arranged referral and follow-up without automatic urgency or fixed timing.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

assessment.text: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped clinical hormone-excess, blood-pressure, hormonal-workup, and electrolyte documentation with qualifiers retained.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

source_limitations: The workflow does not encode all lesion, imaging, biochemical, age, pregnancy, frailty, or malignancy-risk qualifiers.

No-acute-symptom wording, general appearance, weight, weight change, bruising or striae, weakness or fatigue, family history, medicine history, previous records, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, hormone-result interpretation, imaging classification, surgery, treatment, medicine selection, urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
